name: python-executor
description: Run Python code in a sandboxed inference.sh environment with common data, web, and media libraries.
capabilities: Execute Python 3.10 code through the infsh app runner.
Use preinstalled libraries for data analysis, scraping, and visualization.
Perform HTTP requests with common Python HTTP clients.
Write files to outputs/ and return them in responses.
Select the high_memory variant and configure execution timeout.
author: inference-sh
securitySummary: Documentation includes shell command examples for installing and running the CLI, including a pipe to shell installer. These are instructional and not executed by the skill itself, but they warrant caution. Network and crypto related flags appear to be documentation references without malicious intent.
valueStatement: Running Python safely across data, web, and media tasks is difficult when environments differ. This skill runs code in a managed sandbox with common libraries and file outputs.
userTitle: Run Python tasks in a secure sandbox
limitations: CPU only execution with no GPU acceleration or ML runtimes.
Non interactive execution that requires saving plots to files.
Execution time is limited to the configured timeout window.
Requires access to the inference.sh CLI to run the app.

useCases.title: Analyze small datasets
useCases.description: Create tables and charts and return files for review.

useCases.title: Validate scraping logic
useCases.description: Fetch a page and extract specific fields for a report.

useCases.title: Generate demo assets
useCases.description: Produce images or short clips for product demos.

promptTemplates.title: Run a simple check
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute Python to print the pandas version and return stdout only.

promptTemplates.title: Scrape a page title
promptTemplates.prompt: Fetch https://example.com and return the page title as plain text.

promptTemplates.title: Create a chart file
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a small bar chart from sample data and save it to outputs/chart.png.

promptTemplates.title: Process a large dataset
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the high_memory variant, read a CSV, summarize key metrics, and save outputs/summary.csv.

bestPractices: Describe inputs, outputs, and file names explicitly.
Write artifacts to outputs/ so they are returned automatically.
Keep network requests limited to trusted domains.
antiPatterns: Requesting interactive plots or GUI output.
Running jobs that exceed the timeout window.
Asking to install new system packages inside the sandbox.

faq.question: What Python version does the app use?
faq.answer: The app runs Python 3.10 in a sandboxed environment.

faq.question: Can it access the internet?
faq.answer: Yes, the examples show HTTP requests using Python libraries.

faq.question: How do I return files?
faq.answer: Save files to the outputs/ directory and they will be returned.

faq.question: Is there a high memory option?
faq.answer: Yes, use the high_memory variant for larger workloads.

faq.question: Can it run long interactive sessions?
faq.answer: No, execution is non interactive and limited by the timeout setting.

faq.question: Which libraries are available?
faq.answer: The documentation lists many preinstalled libraries for data, web, and media tasks.
